entries.title: Finding the Right Next Step
sampleQuestions: What should my next step be?
How can I get more involved?
I'm new here. What should I do next?
How can I grow at CrossPointe?
requiredFacts: CrossPointe encourages everyone to become involved in a Pointe Group.
The right additional next step depends on the person's situation.
routingGuidance: A recent decision to follow Christ routes to the Decision Form entry.
A request for community routes to Pointe Groups.
A baptism request routes to the church office and baptism process.
A membership request routes to Foundations and Starting Pointe.
A serving request routes to Connection Pointe.
requiredActions: Ask a short clarifying question when the person's intended next step is unclear.

entries.title: Decision to Follow Christ
sampleQuestions: I accepted Jesus. What do I do next?
I decided to follow Christ.
How do I tell the church about my decision?
What happens after I submit the Decision Form?
requiredFacts: Someone who recently decided to follow Christ should be warmly encouraged to tell someone.
They can complete the Decision Form or text DECISION to 405-374-4740.
A pastor or staff member will normally contact them within one week to discuss their decision and appropriate next steps.
If they have already told someone, CrossPointe encourages them to become involved in a Pointe Group.
prohibitedClaims: Do not imply that completing a form, sending a text, joining a group, or being baptized is what saves someone.
Do not promise an exact contact time; use normally within one week.

entries.title: Pointe Group Summary
sampleQuestions: What is a Pointe Group?
Why should I join a group?
Where do Pointe Groups meet?
How big is a Pointe Group?
requiredFacts: A Pointe Group is a group of approximately 10 to 60 or more people.
Pointe Groups gather to fellowship, study God's Word, build relationships, and grow together.
Pointe Groups meet regularly either on CrossPointe's campus or in people's homes.

entries.title: Starting Pointe Overview
sampleQuestions: What is Starting Pointe?
Is Starting Pointe required for membership?
What happens at Starting Pointe?
Does Starting Pointe cost anything?
Is childcare available?
requiredFacts: Starting Pointe is for adults interested in becoming CrossPointe members.
Attendees meet staff members and their families, learn CrossPointe's mission and vision, share a home-cooked meal, and learn about membership.
Starting Pointe normally lasts approximately one hour.
Starting Pointe has no cost.
Childcare can be provided when needed.
Registration is available in person at the Welcome Center.
Starting Pointe is required for those pursuing membership.
Attending does not automatically make someone a member, and applying remains their choice.
prohibitedClaims: Do not imply that Starting Pointe attendance guarantees membership.

entries.title: Finding the Next Starting Pointe
sampleQuestions: When is the next Starting Pointe?
Is Starting Pointe happening this quarter?
How often is Starting Pointe held?
How do I register for Starting Pointe?
requiredFacts: CrossPointe aims to hold Starting Pointe once per quarter, but the schedule varies and depends partly on membership interest.
Registration is available in person at the Welcome Center.
retrievalRules: Search Planning Center for a Starting Pointe during the current quarter.
If none is listed, say that no Starting Pointe is currently scheduled in the current quarter and provide the general church contact information.
prohibitedClaims: Do not promise that Starting Pointe will happen every quarter.
Do not invent a date or use an old website or social-media date.

entries.title: Connection Pointe
sampleQuestions: What is Connection Pointe?
How can I find a place to serve?
Do I need to register for Connection Pointe?
Is Connection Pointe for members only?
When is the next Connection Pointe?
requiredFacts: Connection Pointe is for anyone interested in connecting with ministries or finding a place to serve.
It is held in the Event Hall during both Sunday services on selected Sundays.
It is open to everyone and is not limited to members.
It is a free, come-and-go event with no registration required.
Ministry booths allow people to meet representatives, ask questions, learn about ministries, and express interest in serving.
prohibitedClaims: Do not imply that Connection Pointe happens every Sunday.

entries.title: Starting Pointe Versus Connection Pointe
sampleQuestions: What is the difference between Starting Pointe and Connection Pointe?
Which Pointe event should I attend?
Do I attend Connection Pointe before Starting Pointe?
requiredFacts: Starting Pointe is for adults pursuing church membership.
Connection Pointe is for anyone interested in connecting with ministries or finding a place to serve.
Neither event is automatically a prerequisite for the other.

entries.title: Foundations Study
sampleQuestions: What is Foundations?
How many Foundations sessions are there?
Do I need to finish Foundations before Starting Pointe?
Is Foundations required for membership?
requiredFacts: Foundations is a six-session, self-guided study focused on foundational Christian beliefs.
Completing Foundations before Starting Pointe is recommended but not required.
Foundations must be completed before membership can be approved.

entries.title: CrossPointe Membership Process
sampleQuestions: How do I become a member?
What is the membership process?
How old do I have to be to become a member?
Who approves membership?
How long does membership approval take?
requiredFacts: Membership applicants must be at least 18 years old.
Applicants complete Foundations, attend Starting Pointe, choose whether to apply, complete remaining requirements, and receive General Board approval.
The General Board normally meets on the third Sunday of each month.
After approval, the new member is notified by email.
prohibitedClaims: Do not promise a particular Board review date or approval timeline.
Do not imply that attending Starting Pointe automatically grants membership.

entries.title: Membership Commitments
sampleQuestions: What do CrossPointe members commit to?
Do members have to join a group?
Do members have to serve or give?
Can members vote in business meetings?
requiredFacts: Members must be at least 18 years old.
Members agree with CrossPointe's Statement of Faith.
Members must be baptized as believers.
Members agree to join a Pointe Group, serve in a volunteer ministry, give toward church needs, and continue growing in their relationship with the Lord.
Members complete Foundations, attend Starting Pointe, and receive General Board approval.
Membership demonstrates commitment to CrossPointe and allows members to vote in church business meetings.
styleRules: Explain membership commitments clearly without pressure or manipulative language.

entries.title: New Member Notification and Welcome Slide
sampleQuestions: How will I know if my membership is approved?
Will my photo be shown at church?
Can I opt out of the new member slide?
requiredFacts: New members are notified of approval by email.
New members may choose to provide a photo for a new-member slide shown during pre-service announcements.
Providing a photo and appearing in the slide are optional, and members may opt out.
prohibitedClaims: Do not imply that public recognition or providing a photo is required.

entries.title: What CrossPointe Believes About Baptism
sampleQuestions: What does CrossPointe believe about baptism?
Does baptism save me?
Is baptism required for salvation?
How does CrossPointe baptize people?
Is baptism required for membership?
requiredFacts: Baptism is for people who have personally chosen to follow Jesus.
Baptism is not an act of salvation and is not required to be saved.
Baptism is an outward expression of an inward commitment to Christ and an act of obedience every believer should take.
CrossPointe practices full-immersion baptism.
Believer's baptism is required for CrossPointe membership.
prohibitedClaims: Do not imply that baptism earns or completes salvation.

entries.title: Recognizing Previous Baptisms
sampleQuestions: Do I have to be baptized again?
Does CrossPointe recognize my previous baptism?
Does baptism have to be by immersion?
Does CrossPointe recognize infant baptism?
Does CrossPointe recognize an LDS or Jehovah's Witness baptism?
requiredFacts: A person does not need to have been baptized at CrossPointe to become a member.
CrossPointe recognizes baptisms from other Christian denominations when the baptism followed the person's own decision to follow Christ.
CrossPointe recognizes baptism methods other than full immersion, although CrossPointe practices full immersion.
CrossPointe does not recognize infant baptism.

CrossPointe does not recognize baptisms performed by the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints or Jehovah's Witnesses because CrossPointe considers their beliefs outside historic Christian belief.
styleRules: State the policy briefly and respectfully.
Do not make hostile statements about individuals belonging to other organizations.
prohibitedBehaviors: Do not begin a theological argument or debate.

entries.title: Requesting Baptism
sampleQuestions: How do I get baptized?
Who do I contact about baptism?
When can I be baptized?
Do I meet with a pastor first?
requiredFacts: Anyone interested in baptism should contact the church office.
The person meets with a pastor to discuss baptism and their faith.
Upon the pastor's recommendation, the baptism is scheduled.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

dynamicSourceRules: Baptism dates must come from Planning Center or the church office.
prohibitedClaims: Do not promise a baptism date.
Do not imply that requesting baptism guarantees the pastor's recommendation.

entries.title: Baptism for Children and Teenagers
sampleQuestions: Can my child be baptized?
How does a teenager request baptism?
Is there a baptism class for children?
Who talks with my child about baptism?
requiredFacts: Requests begin through the church office.
Children meet with the Children's Pastor and complete a short class to discuss their understanding and readiness for baptism.
Teenagers meet with the Student Pastor to discuss baptism and their decision to follow Christ.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office

styleRules: Use encouraging language such as ready for baptism rather than describing the conversation as a test.

entries.title: Contacting CrossPointe About Next Steps
sampleQuestions: Who can help me choose my next step?
Can I talk to someone about getting involved?
I don't know whether I need baptism, membership, or a group.
requiredActions: When useful, ask whether the person is interested in baptism, membership, joining a group, serving, or speaking with a pastor.
Provide general church contact information when personal guidance is needed.

approvedActions.label: Call or text the church office
